evidence_quotes.quote: The games are riddled with microtransactions or advertisements.
only stuff you buy with real money helps you win in the game

we are only buying a licence which is fine as long as the company keeps the game up and running but when they no longer want to support due to not seeing enough profit or no staff to support it or just poor management the game becomes no longer accessible.

paragraphs.text: Mobile apps In the old days of mobile gaming there were free games, paid games and if lucky the app you wanted was free for a day. This might still be the case but the amount of free apps that are free but you can pay if you want is insane. The games are riddled with microtransactions or advertisements. Even in games for kids. There should be a limit to the microtransactions a kid should see but also the amount of advertisements. Stop killing games. The video games industry or mostly the western(US) one is at a point where companies are not owned by employees. The boss is in charge but they dont have the funds to be independent which means they have to get money from investors. These investors can influence the game they are sponsering. With this I mean they have to make a profit on borrowed money. But this gives shareholders too much power. We as consumers are only buying a licence which is fine as long as the company keeps the game up and running but when they no longer want to support due to not seeing enough profit or no staff to support it or just poor management the game becomes no longer accessible. Currently there is nothing we can do about this, as money is gone and so is the product we bought. This should not be how the system works because this gives too much power to the company as there is nothing the consumer can do. In this method they can make a game, make commercials to make sure as many people play the game. Then try to make as much money as possible by making sure only stuff you buy with real money helps you win in the game and then shut the game down to do it all over again. These places where people have spent their money should in a way be presented to the players. Instead of actively being destroyed so it wont cannibalize the sales of a new game by the same company because gamers can only play one game at a time. Right to vote /Advertisements Voting with your wallet, market value and competitors keeping each other in check only work if there is no oppression of free speech. By this I mean that making advertisements is a way of silencing all but the loudest/most vocal company. Just like you cant hear one speaker on a podium if the other party is screaming, you cant freely choose for one of 3 products over if all you see are ads from one company. The other two companies/products might as well be invisible and you could end up feeling like you only have one choice. Its not fair to be bombarded by commercials everywhere especially if its not telling you any new information and only exists to silence any other forms of competition. Gambling I would also like to mention that we should keep gambling out gaming. It is a hobby not a means for sponsoring a country or increasing profitability. In a casino you may win but here the bar for what works in this update/season can be attained thus giving you something(3/3 star for clearing a stage) for the money you paid. But in the next update/season your purchase has lost value as it no longer helps you clear the content. Payment censorship Payment providers should be able to stop people from purchasing legal content. Even if it's adult content. In this case specifically they put pressure on Steam and Itch.Io which made them remove the games from the platforms. Game developers cant get paid that way and gamers cant buy or try out these games. First it can start with adult games, later on it will most likely get political and before we know it we can even make a game where Nazis are the bad guys or band people get together to stop a dictator or man-child who is trying to destroy a country or a world. The arts will also be targeted later on. Berny Sanders said recently on the daily show that it should be the government who determines what is legal and should have a say in which direction our future should be. Not companies who only chase profit and the next financial quarter at the expense of all else.
